pr_title: feat: add zero-downtime schema migration for orders table
pr_description: Adds a `fulfillment_date` column to the orders table. Uses the expand-contract pattern: column is added as nullable first (deploy 1), backfill runs async (deploy 2), NOT NULL constraint added after backfill completes (deploy 3). This PR covers deploy 1 only.
diff:
--- /dev/null
+++ b/migrations/versions/0042_add_fulfillment_date.py
@@ -0,0 +1,38 @@
+"""add fulfillment_date to orders
+
+Revision ID: 0042
+Revises: 0041
+"""
+from alembic import op
+import sqlalchemy as sa
+
+
+def upgrade():
+    # Phase 1: add nullable column — safe to run against live traffic
+    op.add_column(
+        "orders",
+        sa.Column("fulfillment_date", sa.DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True),
+    )
+    op.create_index(
+        "ix_orders_fulfillment_date",
+        "orders",
+        ["fulfillment_date"],
+        postgresql_where=sa.text("fulfillment_date IS NOT NULL"),
+    )
+
+
+def downgrade():
+    op.drop_index("ix_orders_fulfillment_date", table_name="orders")
+    op.drop_column("orders", "fulfillment_date")
+
+
+--- a/src/models/order.py
+++ b/src/models/order.py
@@ -18,6 +18,9 @@ class Order(Base):
     id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True)
     user_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False, index=True)
     status = Column(String(32), nullable=False, default="pending")
     created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
     total_cents = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
+    fulfillment_date = Column(
+        DateTime(timezone=True),
+        nullable=True,   # will be NOT NULL after backfill; see migration plan
+    )
